use std::rc::Rc;
use std::cell::RefCell;

// BAD: domain model built around Rc<RefCell<T>>
struct User {
    name: String,
    orders: Vec<Rc<RefCell<Order>>>,
}

struct Order {
    id: u64,
    items: Vec<Rc<RefCell<OrderItem>>>,
    user: Rc<RefCell<User>>, // back-reference
}

struct OrderItem {
    product: Rc<RefCell<Product>>,
    quantity: u32,
}

struct Product {
    name: String,
    price: f64,
}

fn create_order(user: &Rc<RefCell<User>>, product: &Rc<RefCell<Product>>) -> Rc<RefCell<Order>> {
    let item = Rc::new(RefCell::new(OrderItem {
        product: Rc::clone(product),
        quantity: 1,
    }));
    let order = Rc::new(RefCell::new(Order {
        id: 1,
        items: vec![item],
        user: Rc::clone(user),
    }));
    user.borrow_mut().orders.push(Rc::clone(&order));
    order
}
